text: Confusing overlap between --ready-only flag and 'ready' label

Additional confusion: 'gittoc ready' (the subcommand) and 'gittoc l -l ready' (label filter) also mismatch — 'ready' subcommand means structurally unblocked, while '-l ready' means the user-assigned grooming label. Same root cause: the word 'ready' is overloaded across three concepts (subcommand, --ready-only flag, label). All three should be addressed together.

Proposed solution: rename the label convention from 'ready' to 'groomed' in SKILL.md. This is the least disruptive fix — one label rename resolves all three conflicts. The 'ready' subcommand and '--ready-only' flag keep their natural meaning (structurally unblocked), and 'groomed' clearly means 'spec is reviewed and actionable'. Migration: bulk-rename existing 'ready' labels with a one-liner, update SKILL.md suggested labels. No code changes needed beyond the label rename in docs and existing tickets.

the 'groomed' suggestion isn't commonly used. people tend to use 'ready' for that. I'd rather try to stick to standard semantics. label=ready means: it's ready, go do it. I'd rather rename ready command and --ready-only. maybe to 'unblocked' as you named it?

Agreed — 'ready' is the natural label for 'go do it'. Rename the CLI side instead: (1) rename 'ready' subcommand to 'unblocked', add 'ready' as a deprecated alias that warns, (2) rename '--ready-only' to '--unblocked', same deprecation. The 'ready' label keeps its standard meaning. Migration path: aliases let existing muscle memory work while nudging toward the new names. Could drop the deprecated aliases after a version or two.
Ok lets go for label='ready' and command 'unblocked', strike 'list --ready-only' and command 'ready'.
